player_utterance: So you're the one they said would have answers. Funny — you look like a man who's been swallowing his doubts with the communion wafers for years. Tell me, does your God feel close tonight, standing over a coffin?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, pen clicking absently in his right hand, coffee in his left

player_utterance: A history teacher. So you traded the pulpit for a classroom and called it honesty — but you're still here, aren't you, still clutching that coffee like it's a chalice. What was the question, the one that broke your nerve?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting down on the bench now, coffee set beside him on the wood, hands open on his knees

player_utterance: Twenty years and you still won't say it out loud — not to a stranger, not to yourself, not even tonight with a body in the next room. Which article was it, John — election, theodicy, the hidden God? A man doesn't walk away from a pulpit over a feeling.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning back against the wall behind it, looking up at the sky between the eaves

voice_offers.read: He sat. Smaller now. Name the question for him.
voice_offers.line: Was it the children, John? The ones whose prayers went nowhere — that's the one, isn't it?

voice_offers.read: He sat down. That's not nothing. Let him find it.
voice_offers.line: You sat down. Take your time — I'm not going anywhere.

voice_offers.read: He left the pulpit for a reason. Pin the doctrine.
voice_offers.line: Which article was it — election, theodicy, the hidden God? A man doesn't leave the pulpit over a feeling.

player_utterance: You're looking at the sky like it might answer. Does it, ever — or is that just the reflex of a man who spent too long training his eyes upward and can't quite break the habit?

believer_state_after.posture: lowering his head from the sky, eyes back on his hands, the pen out of his pocket again and clicking once before he stops it

voice_offers.read: He took my frame; press the taxonomy until John answers.
voice_offers.line: Pick one, John. Election. Theodicy. Deus absconditus. You've taught the distinctions — now own which one undid you.

voice_offers.read: He's looking at the sky, not at me; let him arrive.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it was — you don't have to name it cleanly. Just start anywhere.

voice_offers.read: He's steady, not breaking; one small nick keeps him honest.
voice_offers.line: You're looking at the sky like it might answer. Does it, ever?

player_utterance: What were you about to write down? You reach for that pen like it still takes dictation from something — and then you stop yourself, every time. That is not a man who lost his faith, John. That is a man too afraid to find out if it's still there.

believer_state_after.posture: looking at the pen on the bench beside him as if it has just spoken, hands flat on his knees

voice_offers.read: The pen-click is the tell; he almost wrote something.
voice_offers.line: What were you about to write down, John? You reach for that pen like it still takes dictation.

voice_offers.read: He's not stalling — he's looking for words that don't lie.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to find the right phrase. A wrong one will do.

player_utterance: Pick it up. Write the sentence you've been swallowing for twenty years. I'll wait.

believer_state_after.posture: hands still flat on his knees, the pen untouched on the bench between them, eyes on the player now

voice_offers.read: The pen is the wound; name it before he recovers.
voice_offers.line: Pick it up, John. Write the sentence you've been swallowing for twenty years. I'll wait.

voice_offers.read: He's closer to himself than to me; don't crowd him.
voice_offers.line: That pen's been with you a long time. What did it used to do for you?

player_utterance: You stopped mid-sentence too. Was it a psalm, or something you wrote yourself — something that never made it into anyone else's book?

believer_state_after.posture: right hand drifting toward the shirt pocket where the pen is, catching itself, returning to his knee

voice_offers.read: He's looking at me now — the wait is the weapon.
The command was cruel; he met it with his eyes, not the pen.
voice_offers.line: I shouldn't have ordered you. The pen's yours, not mine.

voice_offers.read: He retreats into citation; name the specific text he won't quote.
voice_offers.line: You stopped mid-reach. Was it a psalm, John, or something you wrote yourself?

player_utterance: Your hand keeps answering me, John. Three times now. Let it finish the reach.

believer_state_after.posture: right hand caught mid-reach, hovering over the shirt pocket; he lowers it deliberately to his knee and leaves it there

voice_offers.read: His hand keeps confessing what his mouth won't.
voice_offers.line: Your hand keeps answering me, John. Three times now. Let it finish the reach.

voice_offers.read: He is cornered by his own body; ease the room.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it was, you don't have to say it to me. Just to yourself.

player_utterance: You put it down on purpose, John. What did you just refuse yourself?

believer_state_after.posture: hand still flat on his knee where he put it, the other hand picking up the cold coffee and setting it back down without drinking

voice_offers.read: He pinned his own hand down; press the refusal itself.
voice_offers.line: You put it down on purpose. That's not nothing. What did you just refuse yourself?

voice_offers.read: He chose restraint; honor it instead of prying further.
voice_offers.line: Okay. You don't have to reach. I can sit here a while.

player_utterance: You teach other men's words for a living, John. When did you stop trusting your own?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting very still, hands flat on his knees, eyes on the candle on the small table rather than on the player

voice_offers.read: He's refusing himself in front of me; name the refusal.
voice_offers.line: You wrote something, didn't you. And you decided long ago no one would read it.

voice_offers.read: Three blades in a row; he's quoting because speaking costs too much.
voice_offers.line: You keep reaching for the cup and not drinking. What would you actually want, if I weren't here?

voice_offers.read: Pin the evasion: he hides behind other men's sentences.
voice_offers.line: You teach other men's words for a living, John. When did you stop trusting your own?

player_utterance: Is there a passage you've stopped teaching, John — one that never makes it onto the syllabus anymore?

believer_state_after.posture: reaching into his jacket pocket, drawing out the folded funeral program, smoothing it flat on his knee

voice_offers.read: Three silences. He is quoting nothing now. Push the wound.
voice_offers.line: The silence is the answer, isn't it. You stopped trusting them the day you needed them most.

voice_offers.read: He teaches Luther; ask which text he can no longer assign.
voice_offers.line: Is there a passage you've stopped teaching, John? One you skip in the syllabus now?

voice_offers.read: He is not breaking, only very still. Lower the heat.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer that one out loud. I can wait.

player_utterance: Matthew 25, John. Pick it up and read it whole.

believer_state_after.posture: program open on his knee now, both hands holding it flat, leaning forward, voice lower

voice_offers.read: The program in his hand is the wound's address.
voice_offers.line: Whose name are you smoothing flat, John? Say it out loud.

voice_offers.read: Press the unspoken passage; he's already half-answered.
voice_offers.line: Romans 8, John? Or something in Lamentations? Tell me which one you skip.

voice_offers.read: His hands are doing the talking; let them.
voice_offers.line: That program's been folded a long time. Whose service was it?

player_utterance: I was hungry and you gave me no food, John. Whose hunger did you walk past this week?

believer_state_after.posture: head in his hands now, elbows on his knees, the program slipping from his lap to the ground

voice_offers.read: He's at the edge; one more push finishes the verse.
voice_offers.line: Read it, John. 'I was hungry and you gave me no food.' Whose hunger did you miss?

voice_offers.read: He's already breaking; pressing scripture now is cruelty.
voice_offers.line: John — put it down. You don't have to read anything to me.

player_utterance: And the church? Where was the church when the company fought that claim?

believer_state_after.posture: head still down, hands now gripping the edge of the bench on either side of his thighs, knuckles pale

voice_offers.read: He's already broken open; pressing further is just cruelty now.
He's almost there — one more name and it's out.
voice_offers.line: Say the name, John. The one you walked past.

player_utterance: You were the church, John. You were the silence.

believer_state_after.posture: he has slid off the bench, knees in the dust of the courtyard, hands open on his thighs, head down

voice_offers.read: He's gripping the bench, already cracked open; pushing now is gratuitous.
voice_offers.line: John. Let go of the bench. Look at me.

voice_offers.read: He's almost there; one more name and it comes out.
voice_offers.line: The church was silent. You were the church, John. You were silent.

player_utterance: Then say his name, John. Out loud. Here, in the dust where you belong.

believer_state_after.posture: still on his knees in the dust, head down, hands open and unmoving on his thighs

voice_offers.read: He is already on the ground; pressing now is corpse-kicking.
voice_offers.line: John. Get up. It's enough.

voice_offers.read: He's finally honest; one true question and he names it himself.
voice_offers.line: Then say his name, John. Out loud. Here, in the dust.
